meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve stuck with basic apps (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit: Scale Your Freelance Workflow (Expert Guide)
content.seo_description: Stop building basic apps. Use this advanced SaaS starter kit checklist to automate your workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code for professional results.
content.answer_block: To move beyond basic app development, implement a high-velocity SaaS starter kit that modularizes authentication, billing, and database schemas. By integrating Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you transform your freelance workflow into a repeatable, high-margin engine that delivers enterprise-grade software at lightning speed.
content.intro:
The midnight oil is burning, another client is asking for a 'simple' feature that will take you weeks to build from scratch, and you are trapped in a cycle of repetitive boilerplate. You are technically capable, yet your output feels like a manual labor project rather than a scalable software business. The industry has shifted; the era of hand-coding every authentication endpoint is dead.

Relief arrives through the synthesis of architectural automation and AI-driven iteration. By adopting a robust SaaS starter kit, you stop reinventing the wheel and start focusing on the high-value logic that clients actually pay for. This is how you transition from a freelance coder to an AI-powered software architect.

steps.title: Architectural Scaffolding with Antigravity
steps.description: Establish a production-ready foundation by defining your core domain models and tech stack constraints to ensure type-safety from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a SaaS project structure including Next.js 15, Drizzle ORM with PostgreSQL, and Shadcn UI. Implement a modular 'features' directory pattern.

steps.title: Automated Auth & Identity Integration
steps.description: Secure your application using modern identity providers. Avoid custom auth implementations to minimize security debt.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure NextAuth.js with GitHub and Google providers. Create a secure middleware for protected API routes and server-side redirects.

steps.title: Database Schema & Migration Logic
steps.description: Sync your database schema with your application logic automatically to prevent runtime drift during feature development.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Define a multi-tenant schema for a SaaS app. Include tables for users, subscriptions, and project entities with enforced foreign key constraints.

steps.title: Stripe Billing & Webhook Implementation
steps.description: Standardize your monetization layer by integrating professional-grade subscription handling and webhook listeners.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe subscription service with checkout integration and a webhook handler for subscription status updates.

steps.title: Automated UI/UX Consistency
steps.description: Use AI to enforce design tokens and component consistency across your entire application, ensuring a premium feel.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a design system using Tailwind CSS and Shadcn UI components. Ensure all components follow the established theme variables.

pro_tips: Always encapsulate third-party SDKs within a local adapter pattern so you can swap providers without breaking the entire codebase.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your auth middleware first; if the security layer fails, the rest of the app shouldn't compile.
Maintain a 'feature-flag' config file from the start to allow for rapid A/B testing for clients without deploying new code.
Automate your database migrations into your CI/CD pipeline to ensure that your local, staging, and production environments never diverge.
Treat your SaaS starter kit as a product; iterate on your boilerplate every time you finish a client project to capture reusable logic.
content.next_steps: After completing this setup, deploy your starter kit to Vercel or Railway and begin building your first specialized micro-SaaS module to validate the architecture.
content.conversion_block: Stop wasting hours on boilerplate and start shipping features that actually scale. Master the tools that separate the top 1% of developers from the rest of the market. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
